title: Kate-A-Whimsies, John-A-Dreams
content:
Kate-a-Whimsies, John-a-Dreams,
    Still debating, still delay,
    And the world's a ghost that gleams -
    Wavers - vanishes away!

    We must live while live we can;
    We should love while love we may.
    Dread in women, doubt in man . . .
    So the Infinite runs away.

    1876
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Ernest Henley delivers a powerful performance in "Kate-A-Whimsies, John-A-Dreams"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
